weakness_analysis: Massive improvement from v3's 0.083 to 0.762 — v4's Template Enforcement section fixed the structural collapse. All three articles now follow the required template. However, code examples remain weak (0.55). AFTER/fix code blocks frequently use prose descriptions ('Before: single 8994-line file → After: multiple focused files') instead of actual code. The numpy article uses the [Scan data does not include raw source] callout for BEFORE blocks correctly, but the fix sections compensate with only descriptive text, not code. The n8n article has only 2 small inline code snippets despite 6 priority sections. The prompt requires AFTER code to be 'verified-correct or flagged as conceptual' but doesn't explicitly prohibit prose-only fix sections — allowing the model to satisfy the letter of the rule with non-code remediation text.
hypothesis: Add an explicit rule: 'Every Priority section in the Remediation Strategy MUST contain at least one fenced code block — either a BEFORE/AFTER pair or a standalone AFTER example. If BEFORE code is unavailable, write the AFTER fix example anyway. Prose-only remediation steps are not sufficient.' This targets the pattern of high-quality planning steps without demonstrable code.
